section.heading: Child custody protection

subsection.heading: Duration of temporary custody order based on certain deployments

content.p: If a court renders a temporary order for custodial responsibility for a child based solely on a deployment or anticipated deployment of a parent who is a servicemember, the court shall require that the temporary order shall expire not later than the period justified by the deployment of the servicemember.

subsection.heading: Limitation on consideration of member’s deployment in determination of child’s best interest

content.p: If a motion or a petition is filed seeking a permanent order to modify the custody of the child of a servicemember, no court may consider the absence of the servicemember by reason of deployment, or the possibility of deployment, as the sole factor in determining the best interest of the child.

subsection.heading: No Federal jurisdiction or right of action or removal

content.p: Nothing in this section shall create a Federal right of action or otherwise give rise to Federal jurisdiction or create a right of removal.

In any case where State law applicable to a child custody proceeding involving a temporary order as contemplated in this section provides a higher standard of protection to the rights of the parent who is a deploying servicemember than the rights provided under this section with respect to such temporary order, the appropriate court shall apply the higher State standard.

subsection.heading: Deployment defined
subsection.chapeau: In this section, the term “deployment” means the movement or mobilization of a servicemember to a location for a period of longer than 60 days and not longer than 540 days pursuant to temporary or permanent official orders—

paragraph.content: for which dependent travel is not authorized; or
that otherwise do not permit the movement of family members to that location.

section.sourceCredit: (Oct. 17, 1940, ch. 888, title II, § 208, as added Pub. L. 113–291, div. A, title V, § 566(a), Dec. 19, 2014, 128 Stat. 3384.)

note.heading: Codification
note.p: Section was formerly classified to section 528 of the former Appendix to this title prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.
